page: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is a compute-optimized and network-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz, delivering 36 dedicated virtual CPUs across 18 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the c5n.9xlarge demonstrates average tier performance across multiple compute and memory workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1191.0 and a multi-core score of 10778.0, alongside a Passmark CPU score of 30123.3. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1853.99 GB/sec for cached read-only small blocks and 104.3 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For specialized tasks, the system delivers 23541.0 on Geekbench software compilation and 26654.0 on ray tracing benchmarks.

The instance features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, making it highly efficient for network-bound workloads despite the lack of local storage. It achieves 32.41 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and 21.71 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. For language model processing, it handles small 135M models at 1846.91 prompt tokens per second, but performance drops to a weak 5.27 tokens per second for large 70B models. This hardware profile is optimized for network-heavy compute tasks, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c5n.9xlarge is a compute- and network-optimized instance featuring 36 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.67 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on its 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for data transfer. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier, with a Passmark CPU score of 30123.3 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 10778.0. It delivers 32.41 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and 21.71 GB/sec in static web serving. This configuration is designed for network-bound compute workloads, software compilation, and web serving.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86 server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed fifty gigabit baseline network bandwidth for distributed workloads.

96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.67 GB per core
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Average-tier compute performance with a Passmark CPU score of 30123.3
Suited for network-bound compute, software compilation, and web serving
